sections.label: Section 1141
sections.original:
1165
σπεῦσον κοίλην κάπετόν τιν ’ ἰδεῖν
sections.translation: And hear my reply — he shall be buried immediately .

sections.label: Section 1142
sections.original: τῷδ ’, ἔνθα βροτοῖς τὸν ἀείμνηστον
sections.translation: Once I saw a bold-tongued man who had urged sailors to set sail during wintertime . Yet in him you could have found no voice

sections.label: Section 1145
sections.original: πάρεισιν ἀνδρὸς τοῦδε παῖς τε καὶ γυνὴ ,
sections.translation: when the worst of the storm was upon him . No , hidden beneath his cloak he allowed the crew to trample on him at will . And so it is with you and your raging speech — perhaps a great storm , even if its blast comes from a small cloud , will extinguish your shouting .

sections.label: Section 1150
sections.original: κόμας ἐμὰς καὶ τἧςὅε καὶ σαυτοῦ τρίτου ,
sections.translation: Yes , and I have seen a man stuffed with foolishness who exulted in his neighbor ’ s misfortunes . It turned out that a man like me and of similar temperament stared at him and said , Man , do not wrong the dead ; for , if you do , rest assured that you will come to harm . So he warned the misguided man before him . Take note — I see him now , and I think that he is no one but you . Have I spoken in riddles ?

sections.label: Section 1159
sections.original: παρἐστατ ’, ἀλλ ’ ἀρήγετ ’, ἔς τ ’ ἐγὼ μόλω
sections.translation: I will go — it would be a disgrace to have it known

sections.label: Section 1160
sections.original: τάφου μεληθεὶς τῷδε , κἂν μηδεὶς ἐᾷ .
sections.translation: that I argue when I have the power to use force .

sections.label: Section 1161
sections.original:
1185
Τις ἄρα νέατος ; ἐς πότε λήξει
sections.translation: Leave then ! The worst disgrace for me is that I should listen to a fool ’ s empty chatter .

sections.label: Section 1163
sections.original: τὰν ἄπαυστον αἰὲν ἐμοὶ
sections.translation: A trial of this great discord will soon come about . But you , Teucer , with all the speed you can muster ,

sections.label: Section 1165
sections.original: ἄταν ἐπάγων
sections.translation: be quick to seek a hollow grave for Ajax , where he shall establish his dank tomb , a constant memorial for mortals .

sections.label: Section 1168
sections.original: ὄφελε πρότερον αἰθέρα δῦναι
sections.translation: And now just in time his son and his wife approach

sections.label: Section 1170
sections.original: κεῖνος ἀνὴρ , ὃς στυγερῶν
sections.translation: to arrange the burial of the pitiable corpse . Come here , nephew . Take your place near him , and grasp in supplication your father , your begetter . Kneel and pray for help , with locks of hair in your hand from me , her , and thirdly you ;

sections.label: Section 1175
sections.original: ἐκεῖνος οὐ στεφάνων ,
sections.translation: they are the suppliant ’ s only resource . But if any soldier from the army should tear you by violence from this body , then for his wickedness may he be wickedly cast out of his country and get no burial , but be severed at the root with all his race , just as I shear this lock .

sections.label: Section 1180
sections.original: τέρψιν ἰαύειν · ἐρώτων δ ’
sections.translation:
Take it , Nephew , and keep it safe . Let no one move you , but kneel there and cling to the dead .
And you there , do not stand idly by like women , not men . Help defend us until I return , when I have seen to a grave for him , though all the world forbids it .

sections.label: Section 1185
sections.original: λυγρᾶς μνήματα Τροίας . 1210
sections.translation: Which will be the last year ? When will the sum of the years of our many wanderings stop bringing upon me the unending doom of toilful spear-battles

sections.label: Section 1190
sections.original: δαίμονι · τίς μοι , τίς ἔτ ’ οὖν 1215
sections.translation: throughout broad Troy , the cause of sorrow and of shame for Greece ?

sections.label: Section 1192
sections.original: ἵν ’ ὑλᾶεν ἔπεστι πόντου
sections.translation: If only that man had first passed into the depths of the sky or into Hades , the common home of all ,

sections.label: Section 1195
sections.original: τὰς ἱερὰς ὅπως
sections.translation: before he taught the Greeks the shared plague of Ares ’ detested arms ! Ah , those toils of his invention , which produced so many more toils ! Look how that man has ravaged humanity !

sections.label: Section 1199
sections.original:
1225
δῆλος δὲ μοὐστὶ σκαιὸν ἐκλύσων στόμα .
sections.translation: No delight in garlands

sections.label: Section 1200
sections.original: Σὲ δὴ τὰ δεινὰ ῥήματ ’ ἀγγέλλουσι μοι
sections.translation: or deep wine-cups did that man provide me , no sweet din of flutes , that miserable man , or pleasing rest in the night .

sections.label: Section 1205
sections.original: ὅτ ’ οὐδὲν ὢν τοῦ μηδὲν ἀντέστης ὕπερ .
sections.translation: And from love — god !— from love he has totally barred me . Here I lie uncared for , while heavy dews constantly wet my hair ,

sections.label: Section 1210
sections.original: ποίου κέκραγας ἀνδρὸς ὧδ ’ ὑπέρφρονα ;
sections.translation: damp reminders of joyless Troy .

sections.label: Section 1211
sections.original: ποῦ βάντος ἢ ποῦ στάντος , οὗπερ οὐκ ἐγώ ;
sections.translation: In the past bold Ajax was always my bulwark against night ’ s terrors and flying missiles . But now he has become an offering consecrated

sections.label: Section 1215
sections.original: εἰ πανταχοῦ φανούμεθ ’ ἐκ Τεύκρου κακοὶ ,
sections.translation: to a malignant divinity . What joy , then , what delight awaits me anymore ? O to be where the wooded wave-washed cape fences off the deep sea ,

sections.label: Section 1220
sections.original: ἐκ τῶνδε μέντοι τῶν τρόπων οὐκ ἄν ποτε
sections.translation: to be beneath Sunium ’ s jutting plateau , so that we might salute sacred Athens !

sections.label: Section 1223
sections.original: καὶ τοὺς ὄπισθεν εἰς τὸ πρόσθεν ἄξομεν .
sections.translation: Here I am ! I hurried back when I saw the supreme commander , Agamemnon , rapidly approaching .

sections.label: Section 1225
sections.original: οὐδ ’ εὐρύνωτοι φῶτες ἀσφαλέστατοι ,
sections.translation: It is plain to me that he will let his clumsy tongue fly .

sections.label: Section 1226
sections.original: ἀλλ ’ οἱ φρονοῦντες εὖ κρατοῦσι πανταχοῦ .
sections.translation: So it is you , they tell me , who dared open your mouth wide to make fierce threats against us — and are you still unpunished ? Yes , I mean you — you , the captive slave ’ s son . No doubt if you were born from a noble mother ,

sections.label: Section 1230
sections.original: ὁρῶ τάχ , εἰ μὴ νοῦν κατακτήσει τινά .
sections.translation: your talk would reach the sky and you would proudly strut about , when now it is the case that , though you are a nobody and a nothing , you have stood up for this other nothing lying here , and have vowed that we came out with no authority either as admirals or as generals to rule the Greeks or you . No , as an autonomous ruler , you say , Ajax set sail .

sections.label: Section 1235
sections.original: ὅστις πρὸς ἡμᾶς ἀντὶ σοῦ λέξει τὰ σά ;
sections.translation: Does it not shame me that I hear these proud words from slavish mouths ? What was the man whom you shout about with such arrogance ? Where did he advance , or where did he stand his ground , where I did not do the same ? Have the Greeks , then , no other men but him ? To our own harm , it seems , we announced

sections.label: Section 1240
sections.original: φεῦ ! τοῦ θανόντος ὡς ταχεῖά τις βροτοῖς
sections.translation: to the Greeks the contests for the arms of Achilles , if on all sides we are accounted corrupt because of Teucer , and if it will never satisfy you Salaminians , even when you are defeated , to accept the verdict which satisfied the majority of the judges . But instead you will always no doubt aim your slanderous arrows at us ,

sections.label: Section 1245
sections.original: ἀλλ ’ οἴχεται δὴ πάντα ταὖτ ’ ἐῤῥιμμἐνα .
sections.translation:
or treacherously lash at our backs when you fall behind us in the race .
Yet in a place where such ways prevail , there could be no settled order for any law , if we are to thrust the rightful winners aside and bring those in the rear up to the front ranks .

sections.label: Section 1250
sections.original: ἐῤῥύσατ ’ ἐλθὼν μοῦνος · ἀμφὶ μὲν νεῶν
sections.translation: These tendencies must be checked . It is not the stout , broad-shouldered men that are the steadiest allies . No , it is the wise who prevail in every engagement . A broad-backed ox is kept straight on the road all the same when only a small whip directs him .

sections.label: Section 1255
sections.original: ὃν οὐδαμοῦ φὴς οὐδὲ συμβῆναι ποδί ;
sections.translation: And a dose of this very medicine , I foresee , will find you before long , unless you gain a little good sense . He no longer exists , but is already a shade , yet still you boldly insult us and give your tongue too much freedom . Restrain yourself , I say . Recall your birth , your nature .

sections.label: Section 1260
sections.original: ὑγράς ἀρούρας βῶλον , ἀλλ ’ ὅς εὐλόφου
sections.translation: Bring someone else here — a man who is freeborn — who can plead your cause before me in your place . For when you speak , I no longer understand — I do not know your barbarian language .

sections.label: Section 1264
sections.original:
1290
δύστηνε , ποῖ βλέπων ποτ ’ αὐτὰ καὶ θροεῖς ;
sections.translation: If only you both had the sense to exercise self-restraint !

sections.label: Section 1265
sections.original: οὐκ οἶσθα , σοῦ πατρὸς μὲν ὅς προὔφυ πατὴρ ,
sections.translation: There is no better advice that I could give you two .

sections.label: Section 1266
sections.original: ἀρχαῖον ὄντα Πέλοπα βάρβαρον Φρύγα ;
sections.translation: My , how quickly gratitude to the dead seeps away from men and is found to have turned to betrayal , since this man no longer offers even the slightest praise in remembrance of you , Ajax , even though it was for his sake

sections.label: Section 1270
sections.original: λαβὼν ἐπακτὸν ἄνδρ ’ ὁ φιτύσας πατὴρ
sections.translation:
you toiled so often in battle , offering your own life to the spear ! No , your assistance is dead and gone , all flung aside !
Full and foolish talker , do you no longer remember anything of the time when you were trapped inside your defenses ,

sections.label: Section 1275
sections.original: ἴσχει ξύνευνον μητέρ ’, ἣ φύσει μὲν ἦν
sections.translation: when you were all but destroyed in the turn of the battle and he , he alone came and saved you at the moment when the flames were already blazing around the decks at your ships ’ sterns and Hector was leaping high over the trench towards the vessels ?

sections.label: Section 1280
sections.original: οὓς νῦν σὺ τοιοῖςδ ’ ὲν πόνοισι κειμένους
sections.translation: Who averted that ? Was it not Ajax who did it , the one who , you say , nowhere set foot where you were not ? Well , do you grant that he did his duty to you there ? And what about when another time , all alone , he confronted Hector in single combat according to the fall of the lots , and not at anyone ’ s command ?

sections.label: Section 1285
sections.original: θανεῖν προδήλως μᾶλλον ἢ τῆς σῆς ὑπὲρ
sections.translation: The lot which he cast in was not the kind to flee the challenge ; it was no lump of moist earth , but one which would be the first to leap lightly from the crested helmet ! It was this man who did those deeds , and I , the slave , the son of the barbarian mother , was at his side .

sections.label: Section 1290
sections.original: Ἄναξ Ὀδυσσεῦ , καιρὸν ἴσθ ’ ἐληλυθὼς ,
sections.translation: Pitiful creature , how can you be so blind as to argue the way you do ? Are you not aware of the fact that your father ’ s father Pelops long ago was a barbarian , a Phrygian ? That Atreus , your own begetter , set before his brother a most unholy feast made from the flesh of his brother ’ s children ?

sections.label: Section 1295
sections.original: ἄναξ Ὀδυσσεῦ , τοῦδ ’ ὑπ ’ ἀνδρὸς ἀρτίως ;
sections.translation: And you yourself were born from a Cretan mother , whose father found a stranger straddling her and who was consigned by him to be prey for the mute fish . So being of such a kind , can you reproach a man like me for my lineage ? I am the son of Telamon ,

sections.label: Section 1300
sections.original: Οὔ φησ ’ ἐάσειν τόνδε τὸν νεκρὸν ταφῆς
sections.translation: who won my mother for his consort as prize for valor supreme in the army . And she was the daughter of Laomedon , of royal blood , and it was as the flower of the spoil that Alcmena ’ s son gave her to Telamon . Thus nobly born as I am from two noble parents ,

sections.label: Section 1305
sections.original: φίλον σ ’ ἐγὼ μέγιστον Ἀργείων νέμω .
sections.translation: could I disgrace my own flesh and blood , whom even as he lies here subdued by such massive troubles , you , making your pronouncements without a blush of shame , would thrust out without burial ? Now consider this well : wherever you cast him away , with him you will also cast our three corpses .

sections.label: Section 1310
sections.original: κἀμοὶ γὰρ ἦν ποθ ’ οὗτος ἔχθιστος στρατοῦ ,
sections.translation: It is right for me to die before all men ’ s eyes while I am toiling in his cause , rather than for your wife — or should I say your brother ’ s ? With this in mind , then , look not to my safety , but to yours instead , since if you cause me any grief at all , you will soon wish

sections.label: Section 1315
sections.original: Τροίαν ἀφικόμεσθα , πλὴν Ἀχιλλέως .
sections.translation: that you had been more timid than bold when confronting me .

sections.label: Section 1316
sections.original: ὥστ ’ οὐκ ἂν ὲνδίκως γ ’ ἀτιμάζοιτό σοι .
sections.translation: Lord Odysseus , you arrive at the right time , if mediation , not division , is your purpose in coming .

sections.label: Section 1318
sections.original: φθείροις ἄν · ἄνδρα δ ’ οὐ δίκαιον , εἰ θάνοι ,
sections.translation: What is the trouble , friends ? From far off I heard shouting from the Atreidae over this brave man ’ s corpse .

sections.label: Section 1320
sections.original: Σὺ ταῦτ ’, Ὀδυσσεῦ , τοῦδ ’ ὑπερμαχεῖς ἐμοί ;
sections.translation: Is it not because we , Lord Odysseus , have long had to hear the worst , most shameful language from this man ?

sections.label: Section 1322
sections.original: Οὐ γὰρ θανόντι καὶ προςεμβἡναί σε χρή ;
sections.translation: How so ? I can pardon a man a retaliatory barrage of abuse if another has insulted him .

sections.label: Section 1324
sections.original:
1350
Τόν τοι τύραννον εὐσεβεῖν οὐ ῥᾴδιον .
sections.translation: I insulted him , since his conduct toward me was of the same stripe .

sections.label: Section 1325
sections.original: Ἀλλ ’ εὖ λέγουσι τοῖς φίλοις τιμὰς νέμειν .
sections.translation: And what did he do that harmed you ?

sections.label: Section 1326
sections.original: Κλύειν τὸν ἐσθλὸν ἄνδρα χρὴ τῶν ὲν τέλει .
sections.translation: He declares that he will not leave this corpse without due burial , but will entomb it in spite of me .

sections.label: Section 1328
sections.original: Μέμνησ ’ ὁποίῳ φωτὶ τὴν χάριν δίδως .
sections.translation: Then may a friend speak the truth , and still remain your helpmate no less than before ?

sections.label: Section 1330
sections.original: τί ποτε ποιήσεις ; ἐχθρὸν ὧδ ’ αἰδῇ νέκυν ;
sections.translation: Speak . Otherwise I would be less than sane , since I count you my greatest friend among all the Greeks .

sections.label: Section 1332
sections.original: Τοιοίδε μέντοι φῶτες ἔμπληκτοι βροτοῖς ,
sections.translation: Listen , then . In the name of the gods , do not let yourself so ruthlessly cast this man out unburied . Do not in any way let the violence of your hatred overcome you

sections.label: Section 1335
sections.original: Σκληρὰν ἐπαινεῖν οὐ φιλῶ ψυχὴν ἐγώ .
sections.translation: so much that you trample justice under foot . To me , too , this man was once the most hostile enemy in the army from the day on which I beat him for possession of Achilles ’ arms . Yet for all that he was hostile towards me , I would not dishonor him in return or refuse to admit

sections.label: Section 1340
sections.original: ἦ πάνθ ’ ὁμοῖα πᾶς ἀνὴρ αὑτῷ πονεῖ .
sections.translation: that in all our Greek force at Troy he was , in my view , the best and bravest , excepting Achilles . It would not be just , then , that he should be dishonored by you . It is not he , but the laws given by the gods that you would damage . When a good man is dead , there is no justice

sections.label: Section 1345
sections.original: σοὶ μὲν νἐμοιμ ’ ἂν τἧςδε καὶ μείζω χάριν .
sections.translation: in doing him harm , not even if you hate him .
